summary: Full E2E validation of the Bot→Web one-tap auto-login feature. Backend: 9/9 pytest tests pass (config, plans, bot-login endpoint: empty body 400, garbage 401, fresh token 200 with synthetic email tg-<chatId>@bot.local, replay 401, regression). Node unit tests (mint/verify HMAC token): 7/7 pass. Frontend: landing page renders Telegram QR section with correct testids and href, auto-exchange happy path renders dashboard + scrubs ?bt= from URL + populates localStorage store_session, invalid bt shows 'Invalid login link.', replayed bt shows 'This login link has already been used.', mobile 375x667 layout intact, Chinese locale shows '使用 Telegram 继续'. No regressions on theme/lang/unified-login.

backend.POST /api/store/auth/bot-login (replay): 401 'This login link has already been used.'
backend.Token expiry test: Skipped 6-min wait — covered by node unit test 'expired' (crafts past-exp token)

auto_exchange_replay.store-auth-error_text: This login link has already been used. Please request a new one from the bot.

critical_code_review_comments: Token TTL constant verified at 5 minutes via expired-token unit test — not re-tested in live HTTP suite to save time.

Frontend BT_CONSUMED module-level Set successfully prevents React StrictMode double-effect from consuming the token twice in dev (verified — happy path renders dashboard cleanly without 'already used' flash).
history.replaceState scrubs ?bt= from URL after success — verified (final URL = /store with no query).
GET /api/store/config correctly serves without auth header (verified explicitly with no-Authorization request).
test_credentials: Telegram chatId 5346193142 used for bot-login tests; storetest@example.com / password1234 and pnldoctest / 123456 for regression.
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created; existing storetest & pnldoctest accounts already seeded. Cleaned up webBotLoginConsumed + webUsers entries for chatId 5346193142 after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bot→Web auto-login feature is fully working. POST /api/store/auth/bot-login accepts {bt:<HMAC token>}, returns {token, user{email:'tg-<chatId>@bot.local', walletUsd, tgDisplay}}. Tokens are single-use (webBotLoginConsumed collection), 5-min TTL. Frontend Storefront.js auto-exchanges ?bt= on mount via StoreContext.botLogin(), scrubs URL with history.replaceState, persists session to localStorage. Module-level BT_CONSUMED Set prevents StrictMode double-consumption. All 4 locales (en/fr/zh/hi) have the new keys store.orDivider, store.tgButton, store.tgScanCap. To mint a fresh token: TOKEN=$(node -e "require('dotenv').config({path:'/app/backend/.env'}); console.log(require('/app/js/store-routes').mintBotLoginToken('<chatId>'))"). To clean state between retests: db.webBotLoginConsumed.deleteMany({chatId:'<chatId>'}); db.webUsers.deleteOne({tgChatId:'<chatId>', authSource:'telegram'}).
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. Feature implementation matches the spec exactly.
